violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. PIC was filling out food safety manager application at time of inspection. PIC is to send verification of submittal to inspector. New Violation. Correct By: 05/27/2025
Rhode Island's Manager Certified In Food Safety license is expired. FMC must re-instate. Application provided during time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2025

Various foods in the walk-in cooler were observed held at 49-50 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2025

Quaternary ammonium sanitizer solution was in excess of 500 parts per million (ppm). A quaternary ammonia sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label. Sanitizer was adjusted with more water during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2025

Walk-in cooler was observed at an ambient temperature of 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Equipment was taken out of service for potentially hazardous foods. Equipment must be in good repair and proper adjust. Menu limited to cook/serve processes only until walk-in cooler is fixed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/04/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 06/04/2025
